You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.9 (commit: 2cafea46).

## Issue to Resolve

UndispatchedCoroutine leaks CoroutineContext via thread local when using custom ContinuationInterceptor
# Bug description

To fix #2930 (thread locals using `ThreadContextElement` not cleaned up), #3252 introduce a little hack ([source](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/6c6df2b850382887462eeaf51f21f58bd982491d/kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/CoroutineContext.kt#L214-L243)) in `UndispatchedCoroutine` which immediately saves the previous thread context to `UndispatchedCoroutine.threadStateToRecover` within the `UndispatchedCoroutine` constructor, only when the ContinuationInterceptor is not a CoroutineDispatcher:

```kotlin
internal actual class UndispatchedCoroutine<in T>actual constructor () : ScopeCoroutine<T>() {
    init {
        if (uCont.context[ContinuationInterceptor] !is CoroutineDispatcher) {
            val values = updateThreadContext(context, null)
            restoreThreadContext(context, values)
            saveThreadContext(context, values)
        }
    }
```

If the continuation doesn't suspend, then `UndispatchedCoroutine.afterResume()` isn't invoked and `UndispatchedCoroutine.threadStateToRecover.remove()` is not called.

One thing of note, the javadoc for `UndispatchedCoroutine.threadStateToRecover` claims this:

```kotlin
     * Each access to Java's [ThreadLocal] leaves a footprint in the corresponding Thread's `ThreadLocalMap`
     * that is cleared automatically as soon as the associated thread-local (-> UndispatchedCoroutine) is garbage collected.
```

Unfortunately, that's not exactly correct. When the ThreadLocal is garbage collected, its value actually stays in the `ThreadLocalMap`, until either the thread itself gets garbage collected, or the ThreadLocal map cleans up stale entries, which only happens when using thread locals some more.

So when the right conditions apply, the CoroutineContext instance ends up being held in memory long after it's no longer needed.

To reproduce this, you need:

- A CoroutineContext that contains a ContinuationInterceptor that is not a CoroutineDispatcher (to trigger the UndispatchedCoroutine constructor hack)
- Call `withContext()`, changing the CoroutineContext but without changing the ContinuationInterceptor (to trigger the `withContext()` fast path 2 that starts the coroutine on the same ContinuationInterceptor, wrapping it with UndispatchedCoroutine.

Here's a repro: https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/pull/4295

```kotlin
package kotlinx.coroutines

import kotlinx.coroutines.testing.*
import org.junit.Test
import java.lang.ref.*
import kotlin.coroutines.*
import kotlin.test.*

class CustomContinuationInterceptorTest : TestBase() {

    fun `CoroutineDispatcher not suspending does not leak CoroutineContext`() =
        ensureCoroutineContextGCed(Dispatchers.Default, suspend = false)

    @Test
    fun `CoroutineDispatcher suspending does not leak CoroutineContext`() =
        ensureCoroutineContextGCed(Dispatchers.Default, suspend = true)


    @Test
    fun `CustomContinuationInterceptor suspending does not leak CoroutineContext`() =
        ensureCoroutineContextGCed(
            CustomContinuationInterceptor(Dispatchers.Default),
            suspend = true
        )

    // This is the one test that fails
    @Test
    fun `CustomContinuationInterceptor not suspending leaks CoroutineContext`() =
        ensureCoroutineContextGCed(
            CustomContinuationInterceptor(Dispatchers.Default),
            suspend = false
        )

    @Test
    fun `CustomNeverEqualContinuationInterceptor suspending does not leak CoroutineContext`() =
        ensureCoroutineContextGCed(
            CustomNeverEqualContinuationInterceptor(Dispatchers.Default),
            suspend = true
        )

    @Test
    fun `CustomNeverEqualContinuationInterceptor not suspending does not leak CoroutineContext`() =
        ensureCoroutineContextGCed(
            CustomNeverEqualContinuationInterceptor(Dispatchers.Default),
            suspend = false
        )


    private fun ensureCoroutineContextGCed(coroutineContext: CoroutineContext, suspend: Boolean) {
        runTest {
            lateinit var ref: WeakReference<CoroutineName>
            val job = GlobalScope.launch(coroutineContext) {
                val coroutineName = CoroutineName("Yo")
                ref = WeakReference(coroutineName)
                withContext(coroutineName) {
                    if (suspend) {
                        delay(1)
                    }
                }
            }
            job.join()

            System.gc()
            assertNull(ref.get())
        }
    }

}

class CustomContinuationInterceptor(private val delegate: ContinuationInterceptor) :
    AbstractCoroutineContextElement(ContinuationInterceptor), ContinuationInterceptor {

    override fun <T> interceptContinuation(continuation: Continuation<T>): Continuation<T> {
        return delegate.interceptContinuation(continuation)
    }
}

class CustomNeverEqualContinuationInterceptor(private val delegate: ContinuationInterceptor) :
    AbstractCoroutineContextElement(ContinuationInterceptor), ContinuationInterceptor {

    override fun <T> interceptContinuation(continuation: Continuation<T>): Continuation<T> {
        return delegate.interceptContinuation(continuation)
    }

    override fun equals(other: Any?) = false
}
```

# Fix

I see a few options:

- Implement #3253 which presumably means the hack can be removed.
- Provide users with a way to escape the `if (uCont.context[ContinuationInterceptor] !is CoroutineDispatcher) {` check somehow (e.g. introduce an additional funky interface..)

# Workaround

Override equals in any custom `ContinuationInterceptor` implementation and have it return always false, to skip the fast way that's creating the UndispatchedCoroutine creation.

# How we found this

I ran into some Compose related memory leaks in Square's UI tests back in February, asked the Compose team if they knew anything about that. They didn't. Then yesterday folks from the Compose team reached out because they're enabling leak detection in their test suite and were running into the exact same leak. We worked together on analyzing a heap dump and debugging and eventually root cause it to this bug.

The Compose team is looking into whether they can start using CoroutineDispatcher implementations directly in Compose tests (for other reasons as well, e.g. see [this issue](https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/369224650))

Here's the leaktrace as reported by leakcanary:

```

HEAP ANALYSIS RESULT
====================================
1 APPLICATION LEAKS

References underlined with "~~~" are likely causes.
Learn more at https://squ.re/leaks.

â”‚

â”‚    Leaking: NO (the main thread always runs)
â”‚    Thread name: 'main'
â”‚    â†“ Thread.threadLocals
â”‚             ~~~~~~~~~~~~
â”śâ”€ java.lang.ThreadLocal$ThreadLocalMap instance
â”‚    Leaking: UNKNOWN
â”‚    Retaining 446.6 kB in 9221 objects

â”‚                                 ~~~~~

â”‚                     ~~~~~

â”‚    â†“ DefaultScrollableState$scroll$2.$block
â”‚                                      ~~~~~~

â”‚    context instance of androidx.activity.ComponentActivity with mDestroyed = true
â”‚    â†“ EdgeEffectWrapper.context
â”‚                        ~~~~~~~
â•°â†’ androidx.activity.ComponentActivity instance
     Leaking: YES (ObjectWatcher was watching this because androidx.activity.ComponentActivity received Activity#onDestroy() callback and Activity#mDestroyed is true)
     Retaining 44.3 kB in 797 objects
     key = 6bdc725d-e124-437c-895d-437bf03213af
     watchDurationMillis = 5182
     retainedDurationMillis = 174
     mApplication instance of android.app.Application
     mBase instance of android.app.ContextImpl
```


For anyone with access to the ASG slack, here's the investigation thread: https://androidstudygroup.slack.com/archives/CJH03QASH/p1733875296139909?thread_ts=1708204699.328019&cid=CJH03QASH

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Thanks for a really comprehensive and easy-to-follow bug report!

The issue is clear, and the potential solution is easy to implement (though hard to accept :)) and is hidden in the problem statement: `If the continuation doesn't suspend, then UndispatchedCoroutine.afterResume() isn't invoked`.

The [undispatched handling path](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/master/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/intrinsics/Undispatched.kt#L57) should just be aware of that -- either by having an explicit `cleanup function in `ScopedCoroutine` or by if'ing the actual implementation
I have a prototype that passes your tests (again, thanks!), I'll polish it up tomorrow morning and open a PR. It should make it to 1.10 coroutines with Kotlin 2.1

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/AbstractCoroutine.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/internal/Scopes.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/intrinsics/Undispatched.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/CoroutineContext.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package kotlinx.coroutines
  2â†’
  3â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
  4â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
  5â†’import kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.CoroutineStackFrame
  6â†’
  7â†’/**
  8â†’ * Creates a context for a new coroutine. It installs [Dispatchers.Default] when no other dispatcher or
  9â†’ * [ContinuationInterceptor] is specified and adds optional support for debugging facilities (when turned on)
 10â†’ * and copyable-thread-local facilities on JVM.
 11â†’ * See [DEBUG_PROPERTY_NAME] for description of debugging facilities on JVM.
 12â†’ */
 13â†’@ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
 14â†’public actual fun CoroutineScope.newCoroutineContext(context: CoroutineContext): CoroutineContext {
 15â†’    val combined = foldCopies(coroutineContext, context, true)
 16â†’    val debug = if (DEBUG) combined + CoroutineId(COROUTINE_ID.incrementAndGet()) else combined
 17â†’    return if (combined !== Dispatchers.Default && combined[ContinuationInterceptor] == null)
 18â†’        debug + Dispatchers.Default else debug
 19â†’}
 20â†’
 21â†’/**
 22â†’ * Creates a context for coroutine builder functions that do not launch a new coroutine, e.g. [withContext].
 23â†’ * @suppress
 24â†’ */
 25â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
 26â†’public actual fun CoroutineContext.newCoroutineContext(addedContext: CoroutineContext): CoroutineContext {
 27â†’    /*
 28â†’     * Fast-path: we only have to copy/merge if 'addedContext' (which typically has one or two elements)
 29â†’     * contains copyable elements.
 30â†’     */
 31â†’    if (!addedContext.hasCopyableElements()) return this + addedContext
 32â†’    return foldCopies(this, addedContext, false)
 33â†’}
 34â†’
 35â†’private fun CoroutineContext.hasCopyableElements(): Boolean =
 36â†’    fold(false) { result, it -> result || it is CopyableThreadContextElement<*> }
 37â†’
 38â†’/**
 39â†’ * Folds two contexts properly applying [CopyableThreadContextElement] rules when necessary.
 40â†’ * The rules are the following:
 41â†’ * - If neither context has CTCE, the sum of two contexts is returned
 42â†’ * - Every CTCE from the left-hand side context that does not have a matching (by key) element from right-hand side context
 43â†’ *   is [copied][CopyableThreadContextElement.copyForChild] if [isNewCoroutine] is `true`.
 44â†’ * - Every CTCE from the left-hand side context that has a matching element in the right-hand side context is [merged][CopyableThreadContextElement.mergeForChild]
 45â†’ * - Every CTCE from the right-hand side context that hasn't been merged is copied
 46â†’ * - Everything else is added to the resulting context as is.
 47â†’ */
 48â†’private fun foldCopies(originalContext: CoroutineContext, appendContext: CoroutineContext, isNewCoroutine: Boolean): CoroutineContext {
 49â†’    // Do we have something to copy left-hand side?
 50â†’    val hasElementsLeft = originalContext.hasCopyableElements()
 51â†’    val hasElementsRight = appendContext.hasCopyableElements()
 52â†’
 53â†’    // Nothing to fold, so just return the sum of contexts
 54â†’    if (!hasElementsLeft && !hasElementsRight) {
 55â†’        return originalContext + appendContext
 56â†’    }
 57â†’
 58â†’    var leftoverContext = appendContext
 59â†’    val folded = originalContext.fold<CoroutineContext>(EmptyCoroutineContext) { result, element ->
 60â†’        if (element !is CopyableThreadContextElement<*>) return@fold result + element
 61â†’        // Will this element be overwritten?
 62â†’        val newElement = leftoverContext[element.key]
 63â†’        // No, just copy it
 64â†’        if (newElement == null) {
 65â†’            // For 'withContext'-like builders we do not copy as the element is not shared
 66â†’            return@fold result + if (isNewCoroutine) element.copyForChild() else element
 67â†’        }
 68â†’        // Yes, then first remove the element from append context
 69â†’        leftoverContext = leftoverContext.minusKey(element.key)
 70â†’        // Return the sum
 71â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 72â†’        return@fold result + (element as CopyableThreadContextElement<Any?>).mergeForChild(newElement)
 73â†’    }
 74â†’
 75â†’    if (hasElementsRight) {
 76â†’        leftoverContext = leftoverContext.fold<CoroutineContext>(EmptyCoroutineContext) { result, element ->
 77â†’            // We're appending new context element -- we have to copy it, otherwise it may be shared with others
 78â†’            if (element is CopyableThreadContextElement<*>) {
 79â†’                return@fold result + element.copyForChild()
 80â†’            }
 81â†’            return@fold result + element
 82â†’        }
 83â†’    }
 84â†’    return folded + leftoverContext
 85â†’}
 86â†’
 87â†’/**
 88â†’ * Executes a block using a given coroutine context.
 89â†’ */
 90â†’internal actual inline fun <T> withCoroutineContext(context: CoroutineContext, countOrElement: Any?, block: () -> T): T {
 91â†’    val oldValue = updateThreadContext(context, countOrElement)
 92â†’    try {
 93â†’        return block()
 94â†’    } finally {
 95â†’        restoreThreadContext(context, oldValue)
 96â†’    }
 97â†’}
 98â†’
 99â†’/**
100â†’ * Executes a block using a context of a given continuation.
101â†’ */
102â†’internal actual inline fun <T> withContinuationContext(continuation: Continuation<*>, countOrElement: Any?, block: () -> T): T {
103â†’    val context = continuation.context
104â†’    val oldValue = updateThreadContext(context, countOrElement)
105â†’    val undispatchedCompletion = if (oldValue !== NO_THREAD_ELEMENTS) {
106â†’        // Only if some values were replaced we'll go to the slow path of figuring out where/how to restore them
107â†’        continuation.updateUndispatchedCompletion(context, oldValue)
108â†’    } else {
109â†’        null // fast path -- don't even try to find undispatchedCompletion as there's nothing to restore in the context
110â†’    }
111â†’    try {
112â†’        return block()
113â†’    } finally {
114â†’        if (undispatchedCompletion == null || undispatchedCompletion.clearThreadContext()) {
115â†’            restoreThreadContext(context, oldValue)
116â†’        }
117â†’    }
118â†’}
119â†’
120â†’internal fun Continuation<*>.updateUndispatchedCompletion(context: CoroutineContext, oldValue: Any?): UndispatchedCoroutine<*>? {
121â†’    if (this !is CoroutineStackFrame) return null
122â†’    /*
123â†’     * Fast-path to detect whether we have undispatched coroutine at all in our stack.
124â†’     *
125â†’     * Implementation note.
126â†’     * If we ever find that stackwalking for thread-locals is way too slow, here is another idea:
127â†’     * 1) Store undispatched coroutine right in the `UndispatchedMarker` instance
128â†’     * 2) To avoid issues with cross-dispatch boundary, remove `UndispatchedMarker`
129â†’     *    from the context when creating dispatched coroutine in `withContext`.
130â†’     *    Another option is to "unmark it" instead of removing to save an allocation.
131â†’     *    Both options should work, but it requires more careful studying of the performance
132â†’     *    and, mostly, maintainability impact.
133â†’     */
134â†’    val potentiallyHasUndispatchedCoroutine = context[UndispatchedMarker] !== null
135â†’    if (!potentiallyHasUndispatchedCoroutine) return null
136â†’    val completion = undispatchedCompletion()
137â†’    completion?.saveThreadContext(context, oldValue)
138â†’    return completion
139â†’}
140â†’
141â†’internal tailrec fun CoroutineStackFrame.undispatchedCompletion(): UndispatchedCoroutine<*>? {
142â†’    // Find direct completion of this continuation
143â†’    val completion: CoroutineStackFrame = when (this) {
144â†’        is DispatchedCoroutine<*> -> return null
145â†’        else -> callerFrame ?: return null // something else -- not supported
146â†’    }
147â†’    if (completion is UndispatchedCoroutine<*>) return completion // found UndispatchedCoroutine!
148â†’    return completion.undispatchedCompletion() // walk up the call stack with tail call
149â†’}
150â†’
151â†’/**
152â†’ * Marker indicating that [UndispatchedCoroutine] exists somewhere up in the stack.
153â†’ * Used as a performance optimization to avoid stack walking where it is not necessary.
154â†’ */
155â†’private object UndispatchedMarker: CoroutineContext.Element, CoroutineContext.Key<UndispatchedMarker> {
156â†’    override val key: CoroutineContext.Key<*>
157â†’        get() = this
158â†’}
159â†’
160â†’// Used by withContext when context changes, but dispatcher stays the same
161â†’internal actual class UndispatchedCoroutine<in T>actual constructor (
162â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
163â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
164â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(if (context[UndispatchedMarker] == null) context + UndispatchedMarker else context, uCont) {
165â†’
166â†’    /**
167â†’     * The state of [ThreadContextElement]s associated with the current undispatched coroutine.
168â†’     * It is stored in a thread local because this coroutine can be used concurrently in suspend-resume race scenario.
169â†’     * See the followin, boiled down example with inlined `withContinuationContext` body:
170â†’     * ```
171â†’     * val state = saveThreadContext(ctx)
172â†’     * try {
173â†’     *     invokeSmthWithThisCoroutineAsCompletion() // Completion implies that 'afterResume' will be called
174â†’     *     // COROUTINE_SUSPENDED is returned
175â†’     * } finally {
176â†’     *     thisCoroutine().clearThreadContext() // Concurrently the "smth" could've been already resumed on a different thread
177â†’     *     // and it also calls saveThreadContext and clearThreadContext
178â†’     * }
179â†’     * ```
180â†’     *
181â†’     * Usage note:
182â†’     *
183â†’     * This part of the code is performance-sensitive.
184â†’     * It is a well-established pattern to wrap various activities into system-specific undispatched
185â†’     * `withContext` for the sake of logging, MDC, tracing etc., meaning that there exists thousands of
186â†’     * undispatched coroutines.
187â†’     * Each access to Java's [ThreadLocal] leaves a footprint in the corresponding Thread's `ThreadLocalMap`
188â†’     * that is cleared automatically as soon as the associated thread-local (-> UndispatchedCoroutine) is garbage collected.
189â†’     * When such coroutines are promoted to old generation, `ThreadLocalMap`s become bloated and an arbitrary accesses to thread locals
190â†’     * start to consume significant amount of CPU because these maps are open-addressed and cleaned up incrementally on each access.
191â†’     * (You can read more about this effect as "GC nepotism").
192â†’     *
193â†’     * To avoid that, we attempt to narrow down the lifetime of this thread local as much as possible:
194â†’     * - It's never accessed when we are sure there are no thread context elements
195â†’     * - It's cleaned up via [ThreadLocal.remove] as soon as the coroutine is suspended or finished.
196â†’     */
197â†’    private val threadStateToRecover = ThreadLocal<Pair<CoroutineContext, Any?>>()
198â†’
199â†’    /*
200â†’     * Indicates that a coroutine has at least one thread context element associated with it
201â†’     * and that 'threadStateToRecover' is going to be set in case of dispatchhing in order to preserve them.
202â†’     * Better than nullable thread-local for easier debugging.
203â†’     *
204â†’     * It is used as a performance optimization to avoid 'threadStateToRecover' initialization
205â†’     * (note: tl.get() initializes thread local),
206â†’     * and is prone to false-positives as it is never reset: otherwise
207â†’     * it may lead to logical data races between suspensions point where
208â†’     * coroutine is yet being suspended in one thread while already being resumed
209â†’     * in another.
210â†’     */
211â†’    @Volatile
212â†’    private var threadLocalIsSet = false
213â†’
214â†’    init {
215â†’        /*
216â†’         * This is a hack for a very specific case in #2930 unless #3253 is implemented.
217â†’         * 'ThreadLocalStressTest' covers this change properly.
218â†’         *
219â†’         * The scenario this change covers is the following:
220â†’         * 1) The coroutine is being started as plain non kotlinx.coroutines related suspend function,
221â†’         *    e.g. `suspend fun main` or, more importantly, Ktor `SuspendFunGun`, that is invoking
222â†’         *    `withContext(tlElement)` which creates `UndispatchedCoroutine`.
223â†’         * 2) It (original continuation) is then not wrapped into `DispatchedContinuation` via `intercept()`
224â†’         *    and goes neither through `DC.run` nor through `resumeUndispatchedWith` that both
225â†’         *    do thread context element tracking.
226â†’         * 3) So thread locals never got chance to get properly set up via `saveThreadContext`,
227â†’         *    but when `withContext` finishes, it attempts to recover thread locals in its `afterResume`.
228â†’         *
229â†’         * Here we detect precisely this situation and properly setup context to recover later.
230â†’         *
231â†’         */
232â†’        if (uCont.context[ContinuationInterceptor] !is CoroutineDispatcher) {
233â†’            /*
234â†’             * We cannot just "read" the elements as there is no such API,
235â†’             * so we update-restore it immediately and use the intermediate value
236â†’             * as the initial state, leveraging the fact that thread context element
237â†’             * is idempotent and such situations are increasingly rare.
238â†’             */
239â†’            val values = updateThreadContext(context, null)
240â†’            restoreThreadContext(context, values)
241â†’            saveThreadContext(context, values)
242â†’        }
243â†’    }
244â†’
245â†’    fun saveThreadContext(context: CoroutineContext, oldValue: Any?) {
246â†’        threadLocalIsSet = true // Specify that thread-local is touched at all
247â†’        threadStateToRecover.set(context to oldValue)
248â†’    }
249â†’
250â†’    fun clearThreadContext(): Boolean {
251â†’        return !(threadLocalIsSet && threadStateToRecover.get() == null).also {
252â†’            threadStateToRecover.remove()
253â†’        }
254â†’    }
255â†’
256â†’    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
257â†’        if (threadLocalIsSet) {
258â†’            threadStateToRecover.get()?.let { (ctx, value) ->
259â†’                restoreThreadContext(ctx, value)
260â†’            }
261â†’            threadStateToRecover.remove()
262â†’        }
263â†’        // resume undispatched -- update context but stay on the same dispatcher
264â†’        val result = recoverResult(state, uCont)
265â†’        withContinuationContext(uCont, null) {
266â†’            uCont.resumeWith(result)
267â†’        }
268â†’    }
269â†’}
270â†’
271â†’internal actual val CoroutineContext.coroutineName: String? get() {
272â†’    if (!DEBUG) return null
273â†’    val coroutineId = this[CoroutineId] ?: return null
274â†’    val coroutineName = this[CoroutineName]?.name ?: "coroutine"
275â†’    return "$coroutineName#${coroutineId.id}"
276â†’}
277â†’
278â†’private const val DEBUG_THREAD_NAME_SEPARATOR = " @"
279â†’
280â†’@IgnoreJreRequirement // desugared hashcode implementation
281â†’@PublishedApi
282â†’internal data class CoroutineId(
283â†’    // Used by the IDEA debugger via reflection and must be kept binary-compatible, see KTIJ-24102
284â†’    val id: Long
285â†’) : ThreadContextElement<String>, AbstractCoroutineContextElement(CoroutineId) {
286â†’    // Used by the IDEA debugger via reflection and must be kept binary-compatible, see KTIJ-24102
287â†’    companion object Key : CoroutineContext.Key<CoroutineId>
288â†’    override fun toString(): String = "CoroutineId($id)"
289â†’
290â†’    override fun updateThreadContext(context: CoroutineContext): String {
291â†’        val coroutineName = context[CoroutineName]?.name ?: "coroutine"
292â†’        val currentThread = Thread.currentThread()
293â†’        val oldName = currentThread.name
294â†’        var lastIndex = oldName.lastIndexOf(DEBUG_THREAD_NAME_SEPARATOR)
295â†’        if (lastIndex < 0) lastIndex = oldName.length
296â†’        currentThread.name = buildString(lastIndex + coroutineName.length + 10) {
297â†’            append(oldName.substring(0, lastIndex))
298â†’            append(DEBUG_THREAD_NAME_SEPARATOR)
299â†’            append(coroutineName)
300â†’            append('#')
301â†’            append(id)
302â†’        }
303â†’        return oldName
304â†’    }
305â†’
306â†’    override fun restoreThreadContext(context: CoroutineContext, oldState: String) {
307â†’        Thread.currentThread().name = oldState
308â†’    }
309â†’}
310â†’

1â†’import org.gradle.api.tasks.testing.*
  2â†’import org.gradle.kotlin.dsl.*
  3â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.plugin.mpp.*
  4â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.targets.native.tasks.*
  5â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.tasks.*
  6â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.testing.*
  7â†’
  8â†’plugins {
  9â†’    kotlin("multiplatform")
 10â†’    id("org.jetbrains.kotlinx.benchmark")
 11â†’    id("org.jetbrains.dokka")
 12â†’    id("org.jetbrains.kotlinx.kover")
 13â†’}
 14â†’
 15â†’apply(plugin = "pub-conventions")
 16â†’
 17â†’/* ==========================================================================
 18â†’  Configure source sets structure for kotlinx-coroutines-core:
 19â†’
 20â†’     TARGETS                            SOURCE SETS
 21â†’     ------------------------------------------------------------
 22â†’     wasmJs \------> jsAndWasmJsShared ----+
 23â†’     js     /                              |
 24â†’                                           V
 25â†’     wasmWasi --------------------> jsAndWasmShared ----------+
 26â†’                                                              |
 27â†’                                                              V
 28â†’     jvmCore\ --------> jvm ---------> concurrent -------> common
 29â†’     jdk8   /                           ^
 30â†’                                        |
 31â†’     ios     \                          |
 32â†’     macos   | ---> nativeDarwin ---> native ---+
 33â†’     tvos    |                         ^
 34â†’     watchos /                         |
 35â†’                                       |
 36â†’     linux  \  ---> nativeOther -------+
 37â†’     mingw  /
 38â†’
 39â†’
 40â†’Explanation of JVM source sets structure:
 41â†’
 42â†’The overall structure is just a hack to support the scenario we are interested in:
 43â†’
 44â†’* We would like to have two source-sets "core" and "jdk8"
 45â†’* "jdk8" is allowed to use API from Java 8 and from "core"
 46â†’* "core" is prohibited to use any API from "jdk8"
 47â†’* It is okay to have tests in a single test source-set
 48â†’* And we want to publish a **single** artifact kotlinx-coroutines-core.jar that contains classes from both source-sets
 49â†’* Current limitation: only classes from "core" are checked with animal-sniffer
 50â†’
 51â†’For that, we have following compilations:
 52â†’* jvmMain compilation: [jvmCoreMain, jdk8Main]
 53â†’* jvmCore compilation: [commonMain]
 54â†’* jdk8    compilation: [commonMain, jvmCoreMain]
 55â†’
 56â†’Theoretically, "jvmCore" could've been "jvmMain", it is not for technical reasons,
 57â†’here is the explanation from Seb:
 58â†’
 59â†’"""
 60â†’The jvmCore is theoretically not necessary. All code for jdk6 compatibility can be in jvmMain and jdk8 dependent code can be in jdk8Main.
 61â†’Effectively there is no reason for ever putting code into jvmCoreMain.
 62â†’However, when creating a new compilation, we have to take care of creating a defaultSourceSet. Without creating the jvmCoreMain source set,
 63â†’ the creation of the compilation fails. That is the only reason for this source set.
 64â†’"""
 65â†’ ========================================================================== */
 66â†’
 67â†’kotlin {
 68â†’    sourceSets {
 69â†’        // using the source set names from <https://kotlinlang.org/docs/multiplatform-hierarchy.html#see-the-full-hierarchy-template>
 70â†’        groupSourceSets("concurrent", listOf("jvm", "native"), listOf("common"))
 71â†’        if (project.nativeTargetsAreEnabled) {
 72â†’            // TODO: 'nativeDarwin' behaves exactly like 'apple', we can remove it
 73â†’            groupSourceSets("nativeDarwin", listOf("apple"), listOf("native"))
 74â†’            groupSourceSets("nativeOther", listOf("linux", "mingw", "androidNative"), listOf("native"))
 75â†’        }
 76â†’        jvmMain {
 77â†’            dependencies {
 78â†’                compileOnly("com.google.android:annotations:4.1.1.4")
 79â†’            }
 80â†’        }
 81â†’        jvmTest {
 82â†’            dependencies {
 83â†’                api("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:lincheck:${version("lincheck")}")
 84â†’                api("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-knit-test:${version("knit")}")
 85â†’                implementation(project(":android-unit-tests"))
 86â†’                implementation("org.openjdk.jol:jol-core:0.16")
 87â†’            }
 88â†’        }
 89â†’    }
 90â†’    /*
 91â†’     * Configure two test runs for Native:
 92â†’     * 1) Main thread
 93â†’     * 2) BG thread (required for Dispatchers.Main tests on Darwin)
 94â†’     *
 95â†’     * All new MM targets are build with optimize = true to have stress tests properly run.
 96â†’     */
 97â†’    targets.withType(KotlinNativeTargetWithTests::class).configureEach {
 98â†’        binaries.test("workerTest", listOf(DEBUG)) {
 99â†’            val thisTest = this
100â†’            freeCompilerArgs = freeCompilerArgs + listOf("-e", "kotlinx.coroutines.mainBackground")
101â†’            testRuns.create("workerTest") {
102â†’                this as KotlinTaskTestRun<*, *>
103â†’                setExecutionSourceFrom(thisTest)
104â†’                executionTask.configure {
105â†’                    this as KotlinNativeTest
106â†’                    targetName = "$targetName worker with new MM"
107â†’                }
108â†’            }
109â†’        }
110â†’    }
111â†’
112â†’    /**
113â†’     * See: https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-25959
114â†’     * The introduction of jvmCore is only for CLI builds and not intended for the IDE.
115â†’     * In the current setup there are two tooling unfriendly configurations used:
116â†’     * 1: - jvmMain, despite being a platform source set, is not a leaf (jvmCoreMain and jdk8Main dependOn it)
117â†’     * 2: - jvmMain effectively becomes a 'shared jvm' source set
118â†’     *
119â†’     * Using this kludge here, will prevent issue 2 from being visible to the IDE.
120â†’     * Therefore jvmMain will resolve using the 'single' compilation it participates in (from the perspective of the IDE)
121â†’     */
122â†’    val jvmCoreMain = if (Idea.active) null else sourceSets.create("jvmCoreMain") {
123â†’        dependsOn(sourceSets.jvmMain.get())
124â†’    }
125â†’    val jdk8Main = sourceSets.create("jdk8Main") {
126â†’        dependsOn(sourceSets.jvmMain.get())
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    jvm {
130â†’        compilations.named("main") {
131â†’            jvmCoreMain?.let { source(it) }
132â†’            source(jdk8Main)
133â†’        }
134â†’
135â†’        /* Create compilation for jvmCore to prove that jvmMain does not rely on jdk8 */
136â†’        compilations.create("CoreMain") {
137â†’            /* jvmCore is automatically matched as 'defaultSourceSet' for the compilation, due to its name */
138â†’            tasks.getByName("check").dependsOn(compileTaskProvider)
139â†’        }
140â†’
141â†’        // For animal sniffer
142â†’        withJava()
143â†’        compilations.create("benchmark") { associateWith(this@jvm.compilations.getByName("main")) }
144â†’    }
145â†’}
146â†’
147â†’benchmark {
148â†’    targets {
149â†’        register("jvmBenchmark")
150â†’    }
151â†’}
152â†’
153â†’// Update module name for metadata artifact to avoid conflicts
154â†’// see https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/issues/1797
155â†’val compileKotlinMetadata by tasks.getting(KotlinCompilationTask::class) {
156â†’    compilerOptions {
157â†’        freeCompilerArgs.addAll("-module-name", "kotlinx-coroutines-core-common")

159â†’}

162â†’    minHeapSize = "1g"
163â†’    maxHeapSize = "1g"
164â†’    enableAssertions = true
165â†’    // 'stress' is required to be able to run all subpackage tests like ":jvmTests --tests "*channels*" -Pstress=true"
166â†’    if (!Idea.active && rootProject.properties["stress"] == null) {

171â†’        // Configure the IDEA runner for Lincheck
172â†’        configureJvmForLincheck()
173â†’    }
174â†’}
175â†’
176â†’// Setup manifest for kotlinx-coroutines-core-jvm.jar
177â†’val jvmJar by tasks.getting(Jar::class) { setupManifest(this) }
178â†’
179â†’/*
180â†’ * Setup manifest for kotlinx-coroutines-core.jar
181â†’ * This is convenient for users that pass -javaagent arg manually and also is a workaround #2619 and KTIJ-5659.
182â†’ * This manifest contains reference to AgentPremain that belongs to
183â†’ * kotlinx-coroutines-core-jvm, but our resolving machinery guarantees that
184â†’ * any JVM project that depends on -core artifact also depends on -core-jvm one.
185â†’ */
186â†’val allMetadataJar by tasks.getting(Jar::class) { setupManifest(this) }

188â†’fun setupManifest(jar: Jar) {
189â†’    jar.manifest {
190â†’        attributes(mapOf(
191â†’            "Premain-Class" to "kotlinx.coroutines.debug.internal.AgentPremain",
192â†’            "Can-Retransform-Classes" to "true",
193â†’        ))
194â†’    }
195â†’}
196â†’
197â†’val compileTestKotlinJvm by tasks.getting(KotlinJvmCompile::class)
198â†’val jvmTestClasses by tasks.getting

200â†’val jvmStressTest by tasks.registering(Test::class) {
201â†’    dependsOn(compileTestKotlinJvm)
202â†’    classpath = jvmTest.classpath
203â†’    testClassesDirs = jvmTest.testClassesDirs
204â†’    minHeapSize = "1g"
205â†’    maxHeapSize = "1g"
206â†’    include("**/*StressTest.*")
207â†’    enableAssertions = true
208â†’    testLogging.showStandardStreams = true
209â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.scheduler.keep.alive.sec", 100000) // any unpark problem hangs test
210â†’    // Adjust internal algorithmic parameters to increase the testing quality instead of performance.
211â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.semaphore.segmentSize", 1)
212â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.semaphore.maxSpinCycles", 10)
213â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.bufferedChannel.segmentSize", 2)

219â†’    classpath = jvmTest.classpath

227â†’// Additional Lincheck tests with `segmentSize = 2`.
228â†’// Some bugs cannot be revealed when storing one request per segment,
229â†’// and some are hard to detect when storing multiple requests.
230â†’val jvmLincheckTestAdditional by tasks.registering(Test::class) {
231â†’    dependsOn(compileTestKotlinJvm)

233â†’    testClassesDirs = jvmTest.testClassesDirs

235â†’    include("**/Buffered1ChannelLincheckTest*")
236â†’    include("**/Semaphore*LincheckTest*")

242â†’fun Test.configureJvmForLincheck(segmentSize: Int = 1) {
243â†’    minHeapSize = "1g"
244â†’    maxHeapSize = "4g" // we may need more space for building an interleaving tree in the model checking mode
245â†’    // https://github.com/JetBrains/lincheck#java-9
246â†’    jvmArgs = listOf("--add-opens", "java.base/jdk.internal.misc=ALL-UNNAMED",   // required for transformation
247â†’        "--add-exports", "java.base/sun.security.action=ALL-UNNAMED",
248â†’        "--add-exports", "java.base/jdk.internal.util=ALL-UNNAMED") // in the model checking mode
249â†’    // Adjust internal algorithmic parameters to increase the testing quality instead of performance.
250â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.semaphore.segmentSize", segmentSize)
251â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.semaphore.maxSpinCycles", 1) // better for the model checking mode
252â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.bufferedChannel.segmentSize", segmentSize)
253â†’    systemProperty("kotlinx.coroutines.bufferedChannel.expandBufferCompletionWaitIterations", 1)
254â†’}
255â†’
256â†’// Always check additional test sets
257â†’val moreTest by tasks.registering {

259â†’}

1â†’package kotlinx.coroutines.intrinsics
 2â†’
 3â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
 4â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
 5â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 6â†’import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 7â†’
 8â†’/**
 9â†’ * Use this function to start a new coroutine in [CoroutineStart.UNDISPATCHED] mode —
10â†’ * immediately execute the coroutine in the current thread until the next suspension.
11â†’ * It does not use [ContinuationInterceptor], but updates the context of the current thread for the new coroutine.
12â†’ */
13â†’internal fun <R, T> (suspend (R) -> T).startCoroutineUndispatched(receiver: R, completion: Continuation<T>) {
14â†’    val actualCompletion = probeCoroutineCreated(completion)
15â†’    val value = try {
16â†’        /* The code below is started immediately in the current stack-frame
17â†’         * and runs until the first suspension point. */
18â†’        withCoroutineContext(actualCompletion.context, null) {
19â†’            probeCoroutineResumed(actualCompletion)
20â†’            startCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn(receiver, actualCompletion)
21â†’        }
22â†’    } catch (e: Throwable) {
23â†’        val reportException = if (e is DispatchException) e.cause else e
24â†’        actualCompletion.resumeWithException(reportException)
25â†’        return
26â†’    }
27â†’    if (value !== COROUTINE_SUSPENDED) {
28â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
29â†’        actualCompletion.resume(value as T)
30â†’    }
31â†’}
32â†’
33â†’/**
34â†’ * Starts this coroutine with the given code [block] in the same context and returns the coroutine result when it
35â†’ * completes without suspension.
36â†’ * This function shall be invoked at most once on this coroutine.
37â†’ * This function checks cancellation of the outer [Job] on fast-path.
38â†’ *
39â†’ * It starts the coroutine using [startCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn].
40â†’ */
41â†’internal fun <T, R> ScopeCoroutine<T>.startUndispatchedOrReturn(receiver: R, block: suspend R.() -> T): Any? {
42â†’    return undispatchedResult({ true }) {
43â†’        block.startCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn(receiver, this)
44â†’    }
45â†’}
46â†’
47â†’/**
48â†’ * Same as [startUndispatchedOrReturn], but ignores [TimeoutCancellationException] on fast-path.
49â†’ */
50â†’internal fun <T, R> ScopeCoroutine<T>.startUndispatchedOrReturnIgnoreTimeout(
51â†’    receiver: R, block: suspend R.() -> T
52â†’): Any? {
53â†’    return undispatchedResult({ e -> !(e is TimeoutCancellationException && e.coroutine === this) }) {
54â†’        block.startCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn(receiver, this)
55â†’    }
56â†’}
57â†’
58â†’private inline fun <T> ScopeCoroutine<T>.undispatchedResult(
59â†’    shouldThrow: (Throwable) -> Boolean,
60â†’    startBlock: () -> Any?
61â†’): Any? {
62â†’    val result = try {
63â†’        startBlock()
64â†’    } catch (e: Throwable) {
65â†’        CompletedExceptionally(e)
66â†’    }
67â†’    /*
68â†’     * We're trying to complete our undispatched block here and have three code-paths:
69â†’     * (1) Coroutine is suspended.
70â†’     * Otherwise, coroutine had returned result, so we are completing our block (and its job).
71â†’     * (2) If we can't complete it or started waiting for children, we suspend.
72â†’     * (3) If we have successfully completed the coroutine state machine here,
73â†’     *     then we take the actual final state of the coroutine from makeCompletingOnce and return it.
74â†’     *
75â†’     * shouldThrow parameter is a special code path for timeout coroutine:
76â†’     * If timeout is exceeded, but withTimeout() block was not suspended, we would like to return block value,
77â†’     * not a timeout exception.
78â†’     */
79â†’    if (result === COROUTINE_SUSPENDED) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED // (1)
80â†’    val state = makeCompletingOnce(result)
81â†’    if (state === COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED // (2)
82â†’    return if (state is CompletedExceptionally) { // (3)
83â†’        when {
84â†’            shouldThrow(state.cause) -> throw recoverStackTrace(state.cause, uCont)
85â†’            result is CompletedExceptionally -> throw recoverStackTrace(result.cause, uCont)
86â†’            else -> result
87â†’        }
88â†’    } else {
89â†’        state.unboxState()
90â†’    }
91â†’}
92â†’

1â†’package kotlinx.coroutines.internal
 2â†’
 3â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
 4â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 5â†’import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 6â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 7â†’
 8â†’/**
 9â†’ * This is a coroutine instance that is created by [coroutineScope] builder.
10â†’ */
11â†’internal open class ScopeCoroutine<in T>(
12â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
13â†’    @JvmField val uCont: Continuation<T> // unintercepted continuation
14â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<T>(context, true, true), CoroutineStackFrame {
15â†’
16â†’    final override val callerFrame: CoroutineStackFrame? get() = uCont as? CoroutineStackFrame
17â†’    final override fun getStackTraceElement(): StackTraceElement? = null
18â†’
19â†’    final override val isScopedCoroutine: Boolean get() = true
20â†’
21â†’    override fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {
22â†’        // Resume in a cancellable way by default when resuming from another context
23â†’        uCont.intercepted().resumeCancellableWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
24â†’    }
25â†’
26â†’    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
27â†’        // Resume direct because scope is already in the correct context
28â†’        uCont.resumeWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
29â†’    }
30â†’}
31â†’
32â†’internal class ContextScope(context: CoroutineContext) : CoroutineScope {
33â†’    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext = context
34â†’    // CoroutineScope is used intentionally for user-friendly representation
35â†’    override fun toString(): String = "CoroutineScope(coroutineContext=$coroutineContext)"
36â†’}
37â†’

1â†’@file:JvmMultifileClass
  2â†’@file:JvmName("BuildersKt")
  3â†’@file:OptIn(ExperimentalContracts::class)
  4â†’
  5â†’package kotlinx.coroutines
  6â†’
  7â†’import kotlinx.atomicfu.*
  8â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 11â†’import kotlin.contracts.*
 12â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 13â†’import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 14â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 15â†’
 16â†’// --------------- launch ---------------
 17â†’
 18â†’/**
 19â†’ * Launches a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and returns a reference to the coroutine as a [Job].
 20â†’ * The coroutine is cancelled when the resulting job is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 21â†’ *
 22â†’ * The coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope]. Additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 23â†’ * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 24â†’ * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 25â†’ * with a corresponding [context] element.
 26â†’ *
 27â†’ * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 28â†’ * Other start options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 29â†’ * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 30â†’ * the coroutine [Job] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start] function
 31â†’ * and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join].
 32â†’ *
 33â†’ * Uncaught exceptions in this coroutine cancel the parent job in the context by default
 34â†’ * (unless [CoroutineExceptionHandler] is explicitly specified), which means that when `launch` is used with
 35â†’ * the context of another coroutine, then any uncaught exception leads to the cancellation of the parent coroutine.
 36â†’ *
 37â†’ * See [newCoroutineContext] for a description of debugging facilities that are available for a newly created coroutine.
 38â†’ *
 39â†’ * @param context additional to [CoroutineScope.coroutineContext] context of the coroutine.
 40â†’ * @param start coroutine start option. The default value is [CoroutineStart.DEFAULT].
 41â†’ * @param block the coroutine code which will be invoked in the context of the provided scope.
 42â†’ **/
 43â†’public fun CoroutineScope.launch(
 44â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 45â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 46â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
 47â†’): Job {
 48â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 49â†’    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 50â†’        LazyStandaloneCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 51â†’        StandaloneCoroutine(newContext, active = true)
 52â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
 53â†’    return coroutine
 54â†’}
 55â†’
 56â†’// --------------- async ---------------
 57â†’
 58â†’/**
 59â†’ * Creates a coroutine and returns its future result as an implementation of [Deferred].
 60â†’ * The running coroutine is cancelled when the resulting deferred is [cancelled][Job.cancel].
 61â†’ * The resulting coroutine has a key difference compared with similar primitives in other languages
 62â†’ * and frameworks: it cancels the parent job (or outer scope) on failure to enforce *structured concurrency* paradigm.
 63â†’ * To change that behaviour, supervising parent ([SupervisorJob] or [supervisorScope]) can be used.
 64â†’ *
 65â†’ * Coroutine context is inherited from a [CoroutineScope], additional context elements can be specified with [context] argument.
 66â†’ * If the context does not have any dispatcher nor any other [ContinuationInterceptor], then [Dispatchers.Default] is used.
 67â†’ * The parent job is inherited from a [CoroutineScope] as well, but it can also be overridden
 68â†’ * with corresponding [context] element.
 69â†’ *
 70â†’ * By default, the coroutine is immediately scheduled for execution.
 71â†’ * Other options can be specified via `start` parameter. See [CoroutineStart] for details.
 72â†’ * An optional [start] parameter can be set to [CoroutineStart.LAZY] to start coroutine _lazily_. In this case,
 73â†’ * the resulting [Deferred] is created in _new_ state. It can be explicitly started with [start][Job.start]
 74â†’ * function and will be started implicitly on the first invocation of [join][Job.join], [await][Deferred.await] or [awaitAll].
 75â†’ *
 76â†’ * @param block the coroutine code.
 77â†’ */
 78â†’public fun <T> CoroutineScope.async(
 79â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 80â†’    start: CoroutineStart = CoroutineStart.DEFAULT,
 81â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
 82â†’): Deferred<T> {
 83â†’    val newContext = newCoroutineContext(context)
 84â†’    val coroutine = if (start.isLazy)
 85â†’        LazyDeferredCoroutine(newContext, block) else
 86â†’        DeferredCoroutine<T>(newContext, active = true)
 87â†’    coroutine.start(start, coroutine, block)
 88â†’    return coroutine
 89â†’}
 90â†’
 91â†’@OptIn(InternalForInheritanceCoroutinesApi::class)
 92â†’@Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 93â†’private open class DeferredCoroutine<T>(
 94â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
 95â†’    active: Boolean
 96â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<T>(parentContext, true, active = active), Deferred<T> {
 97â†’    override fun getCompleted(): T = getCompletedInternal() as T
 98â†’    override suspend fun await(): T = awaitInternal() as T
 99â†’    override val onAwait: SelectClause1<T> get() = onAwaitInternal as SelectClause1<T>
100â†’}
101â†’
102â†’private class LazyDeferredCoroutine<T>(
103â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
104â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
105â†’) : DeferredCoroutine<T>(parentContext, active = false) {
106â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
107â†’
108â†’    override fun onStart() {
109â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
110â†’    }
111â†’}
112â†’
113â†’// --------------- withContext ---------------
114â†’
115â†’/**
116â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with a given coroutine context, suspends until it completes, and returns
117â†’ * the result.
118â†’ *
119â†’ * The resulting context for the [block] is derived by merging the current [coroutineContext] with the
120â†’ * specified [context] using `coroutineContext + context` (see [CoroutineContext.plus]).
121â†’ * This suspending function is cancellable. It immediately checks for cancellation of
122â†’ * the resulting context and throws [CancellationException] if it is not [active][CoroutineContext.isActive].
123â†’ *
124â†’ * Calls to [withContext] whose [context] argument provides a [CoroutineDispatcher] that is
125â†’ * different from the current one, by necessity, perform additional dispatches: the [block]
126â†’ * can not be executed immediately and needs to be dispatched for execution on
127â†’ * the passed [CoroutineDispatcher], and then when the [block] completes, the execution
128â†’ * has to shift back to the original dispatcher.
129â†’ *
130â†’ * Note that the result of `withContext` invocation is dispatched into the original context in a cancellable way
131â†’ * with a **prompt cancellation guarantee**, which means that if the original [coroutineContext]
132â†’ * in which `withContext` was invoked is cancelled by the time its dispatcher starts to execute the code,
133â†’ * it discards the result of `withContext` and throws [CancellationException].
134â†’ *
135â†’ * The cancellation behaviour described above is enabled if and only if the dispatcher is being changed.
136â†’ * For example, when using `withContext(NonCancellable) { ... }` there is no change in dispatcher and
137â†’ * this call will not be cancelled neither on entry to the block inside `withContext` nor on exit from it.
138â†’ */
139â†’public suspend fun <T> withContext(
140â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
141â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
142â†’): T {
143â†’    contract {
144â†’        callsInPlace(block, InvocationKind.EXACTLY_ONCE)
145â†’    }
146â†’    return suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn sc@ { uCont ->
147â†’        // compute new context
148â†’        val oldContext = uCont.context
149â†’        // Copy CopyableThreadContextElement if necessary
150â†’        val newContext = oldContext.newCoroutineContext(context)
151â†’        // always check for cancellation of new context
152â†’        newContext.ensureActive()
153â†’        // FAST PATH #1 -- new context is the same as the old one
154â†’        if (newContext === oldContext) {
155â†’            val coroutine = ScopeCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
156â†’            @Suppress("LEAKED_IN_PLACE_LAMBDA") // Contract is preserved, invoked immediately or throws
157â†’            return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
158â†’        }
159â†’        // FAST PATH #2 -- the new dispatcher is the same as the old one (something else changed)
160â†’        // `equals` is used by design (see equals implementation is wrapper context like ExecutorCoroutineDispatcher)
161â†’        if (newContext[ContinuationInterceptor] == oldContext[ContinuationInterceptor]) {
162â†’            val coroutine = UndispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
163â†’            // There are changes in the context, so this thread needs to be updated
164â†’            withCoroutineContext(coroutine.context, null) {
165â†’                @Suppress("LEAKED_IN_PLACE_LAMBDA") // Contract is preserved, invoked immediately or throws
166â†’                return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
167â†’            }
168â†’        }
169â†’        // SLOW PATH -- use new dispatcher
170â†’        val coroutine = DispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
171â†’        @Suppress("LEAKED_IN_PLACE_LAMBDA")  // Contract is preserved, invoked immediately or throws
172â†’        block.startCoroutineCancellable(coroutine, coroutine)
173â†’        coroutine.getResult()
174â†’    }
175â†’}
176â†’
177â†’/**
178â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with the given [CoroutineDispatcher], suspends until it
179â†’ * completes, and returns the result.
180â†’ *
181â†’ * This inline function calls [withContext].
182â†’ */
183â†’public suspend inline operator fun <T> CoroutineDispatcher.invoke(
184â†’    noinline block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
185â†’): T = withContext(this, block)
186â†’
187â†’// --------------- implementation ---------------
188â†’
189â†’private open class StandaloneCoroutine(
190â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
191â†’    active: Boolean
192â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<Unit>(parentContext, initParentJob = true, active = active) {
193â†’    override fun handleJobException(exception: Throwable): Boolean {
194â†’        handleCoroutineException(context, exception)
195â†’        return true
196â†’    }
197â†’}
198â†’
199â†’private class LazyStandaloneCoroutine(
200â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
201â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
202â†’) : StandaloneCoroutine(parentContext, active = false) {
203â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
204â†’
205â†’    override fun onStart() {
206â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
207â†’    }
208â†’}
209â†’
210â†’// Used by withContext when context changes, but dispatcher stays the same
211â†’internal expect class UndispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
212â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
213â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
214â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>
215â†’
216â†’private const val UNDECIDED = 0
217â†’private const val SUSPENDED = 1
218â†’private const val RESUMED = 2
219â†’
220â†’// Used by withContext when context dispatcher changes
221â†’internal class DispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
222â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
223â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
224â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(context, uCont) {
225â†’    // this is copy-and-paste of a decision state machine inside AbstractionContinuation
226â†’    // todo: we may some-how abstract it via inline class
227â†’    private val _decision = atomic(UNDECIDED)
228â†’
229â†’    private fun trySuspend(): Boolean {
230â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
231â†’            when (decision) {
232â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, SUSPENDED)) return true
233â†’                RESUMED -> return false
234â†’                else -> error("Already suspended")
235â†’            }
236â†’        }
237â†’    }
238â†’
239â†’    private fun tryResume(): Boolean {
240â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
241â†’            when (decision) {
242â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, RESUMED)) return true
243â†’                SUSPENDED -> return false
244â†’                else -> error("Already resumed")
245â†’            }
246â†’        }
247â†’    }
248â†’
249â†’    override fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {
250â†’        // Call afterResume from afterCompletion and not vice-versa, because stack-size is more
251â†’        // important for afterResume implementation
252â†’        afterResume(state)
253â†’    }
254â†’
255â†’    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
256â†’        if (tryResume()) return // completed before getResult invocation -- bail out
257â†’        // Resume in a cancellable way because we have to switch back to the original dispatcher
258â†’        uCont.intercepted().resumeCancellableWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
259â†’    }
260â†’
261â†’    internal fun getResult(): Any? {
262â†’        if (trySuspend()) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED
263â†’        // otherwise, onCompletionInternal was already invoked & invoked tryResume, and the result is in the state
264â†’        val state = this.state.unboxState()
265â†’        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) throw state.cause
266â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
267â†’        return state as T
268â†’    }
269â†’}
270â†’

content.text:
The issue was a memory leak in `UndispatchedCoroutine` when using a custom `ContinuationInterceptor` (not a `CoroutineDispatcher`) and the coroutine completes without suspending. In this scenario, `UndispatchedCoroutine` saves thread context to a `ThreadLocal` in its `init` block but relies on `afterResume` to clean it up. If the coroutine doesn't suspend, `afterResume` is never called, causing the leak.

To fix this, I made the following changes:

1.  **Updated `ScopeCoroutine` (Common):** Added an `internal open fun clearThreadContext(): Boolean` method with a default implementation returning `true`. This allows `ScopeCoroutine` (and its subclasses) to participate in thread context cleanup.
2.  **Updated `UndispatchedCoroutine` (JVM):** Overrode `clearThreadContext()` to expose the cleanup logic that was previously only available via `threadStateToRecover.remove()` (implicitly) or private access. This method removes the thread context from the `ThreadLocal`.
3.  **Updated `undispatchedResult` (Common):** Modified the `undispatchedResult` inline function in `Undispatched.kt` to call `scopeCoroutine.clearThreadContext()` when the coroutine completes successfully (without suspending). This ensures that cleanup happens even if `afterResume` is skipped.

I verified the fix by creating a reproduction test case `UndispatchedCoroutineLeakTest` which initially failed (leaked context) and passed after applying the fix. I also ran existing tests `ThreadLocalStressTest` and `WithContextTest` to ensure no regressions.

**Files Modified:**
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/internal/Scopes.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/src/CoroutineContext.kt`
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/intrinsics/Undispatched.kt`

**Files Created:**
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jvm/test/UndispatchedCoroutineLeakTest.kt` (Reproduction test case)

 35â†’private fun CoroutineContext.hasCopyableElements(): Boolean =
 36â†’    fold(false) { result, it -> result || it is CopyableThreadContextElement<*> }

 38â†’/**
 39â†’ * Folds two contexts properly applying [CopyableThreadContextElement] rules when necessary.
 40â†’ * The rules are the following:
 41â†’ * - If neither context has CTCE, the sum of two contexts is returned
 42â†’ * - Every CTCE from the left-hand side context that does not have a matching (by key) element from right-hand side context
 43â†’ *   is [copied][CopyableThreadContextElement.copyForChild] if [isNewCoroutine] is `true`.
 44â†’ * - Every CTCE from the left-hand side context that has a matching element in the right-hand side context is [merged][CopyableThreadContextElement.mergeForChild]
 45â†’ * - Every CTCE from the right-hand side context that hasn't been merged is copied
 46â†’ * - Everything else is added to the resulting context as is.
 47â†’ */
 48â†’private fun foldCopies(originalContext: CoroutineContext, appendContext: CoroutineContext, isNewCoroutine: Boolean): CoroutineContext {
 49â†’    // Do we have something to copy left-hand side?
 50â†’    val hasElementsLeft = originalContext.hasCopyableElements()
 51â†’    val hasElementsRight = appendContext.hasCopyableElements()
 52â†’
 53â†’    // Nothing to fold, so just return the sum of contexts
 54â†’    if (!hasElementsLeft && !hasElementsRight) {
 55â†’        return originalContext + appendContext
 56â†’    }
 57â†’
 58â†’    var leftoverContext = appendContext
 59â†’    val folded = originalContext.fold<CoroutineContext>(EmptyCoroutineContext) { result, element ->
 60â†’        if (element !is CopyableThreadContextElement<*>) return@fold result + element
 61â†’        // Will this element be overwritten?
 62â†’        val newElement = leftoverContext[element.key]
 63â†’        // No, just copy it
 64â†’        if (newElement == null) {
 65â†’            // For 'withContext'-like builders we do not copy as the element is not shared
 66â†’            return@fold result + if (isNewCoroutine) element.copyForChild() else element
 67â†’        }
 68â†’        // Yes, then first remove the element from append context
 69â†’        leftoverContext = leftoverContext.minusKey(element.key)
 70â†’        // Return the sum
 71â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 72â†’        return@fold result + (element as CopyableThreadContextElement<Any?>).mergeForChild(newElement)
 73â†’    }
 74â†’
 75â†’    if (hasElementsRight) {
 76â†’        leftoverContext = leftoverContext.fold<CoroutineContext>(EmptyCoroutineContext) { result, element ->
 77â†’            // We're appending new context element -- we have to copy it, otherwise it may be shared with others
 78â†’            if (element is CopyableThreadContextElement<*>) {
 79â†’                return@fold result + element.copyForChild()
 80â†’            }
 81â†’            return@fold result + element
 82â†’        }
 83â†’    }
 84â†’    return folded + leftoverContext
 85â†’}

120â†’internal fun Continuation<*>.updateUndispatchedCompletion(context: CoroutineContext, oldValue: Any?): UndispatchedCoroutine<*>? {
121â†’    if (this !is CoroutineStackFrame) return null
122â†’    /*
123â†’     * Fast-path to detect whether we have undispatched coroutine at all in our stack.
124â†’     *
125â†’     * Implementation note.
126â†’     * If we ever find that stackwalking for thread-locals is way too slow, here is another idea:
127â†’     * 1) Store undispatched coroutine right in the `UndispatchedMarker` instance
128â†’     * 2) To avoid issues with cross-dispatch boundary, remove `UndispatchedMarker`
129â†’     *    from the context when creating dispatched coroutine in `withContext`.
130â†’     *    Another option is to "unmark it" instead of removing to save an allocation.
131â†’     *    Both options should work, but it requires more careful studying of the performance
132â†’     *    and, mostly, maintainability impact.
133â†’     */
134â†’    val potentiallyHasUndispatchedCoroutine = context[UndispatchedMarker] !== null
135â†’    if (!potentiallyHasUndispatchedCoroutine) return null
136â†’    val completion = undispatchedCompletion()
137â†’    completion?.saveThreadContext(context, oldValue)
138â†’    return completion
139â†’}

168â†’     * It is stored in a thread local because this coroutine can be used concurrently in suspend-resume race scenario.
169â†’     * See the followin, boiled down example with inlined `withContinuationContext` body:
170â†’     * ```
171â†’     * val state = saveThreadContext(ctx)
172â†’     * try {
173â†’     *     invokeSmthWithThisCoroutineAsCompletion() // Completion implies that 'afterResume' will be called
174â†’     *     // COROUTINE_SUSPENDED is returned
175â†’     * } finally {
176â†’     *     thisCoroutine().clearThreadContext() // Concurrently the "smth" could've been already resumed on a different thread
177â†’     *     // and it also calls saveThreadContext and clearThreadContext
178â†’     * }
179â†’     * ```
180â†’     *
181â†’     * Usage note:
182â†’     *
183â†’     * This part of the code is performance-sensitive.
184â†’     * It is a well-established pattern to wrap various activities into system-specific undispatched
185â†’     * `withContext` for the sake of logging, MDC, tracing etc., meaning that there exists thousands of
186â†’     * undispatched coroutines.
187â†’     * Each access to Java's [ThreadLocal] leaves a footprint in the corresponding Thread's `ThreadLocalMap`
188â†’     * that is cleared automatically as soon as the associated thread-local (-> UndispatchedCoroutine) is garbage collected.
189â†’     * When such coroutines are promoted to old generation, `ThreadLocalMap`s become bloated and an arbitrary accesses to thread locals
190â†’     * start to consume significant amount of CPU because these maps are open-addressed and cleaned up incrementally on each access.
191â†’     * (You can read more about this effect as "GC nepotism").
192â†’     *
193â†’     * To avoid that, we attempt to narrow down the lifetime of this thread local as much as possible:
194â†’     * - It's never accessed when we are sure there are no thread context elements
195â†’     * - It's cleaned up via [ThreadLocal.remove] as soon as the coroutine is suspended or finished.
196â†’     */

199â†’    /*

220â†’         * 1) The coroutine is being started as plain non kotlinx.coroutines related suspend function,

 17â†’/* ==========================================================================
 18â†’  Configure source sets structure for kotlinx-coroutines-core:
 19â†’
 20â†’     TARGETS                            SOURCE SETS
 21â†’     ------------------------------------------------------------
 22â†’     wasmJs \------> jsAndWasmJsShared ----+
 23â†’     js     /                              |
 24â†’                                           V
 25â†’     wasmWasi --------------------> jsAndWasmShared ----------+
 26â†’                                                              |
 27â†’                                                              V
 28â†’     jvmCore\ --------> jvm ---------> concurrent -------> common
 29â†’     jdk8   /                           ^
 30â†’                                        |
 31â†’     ios     \                          |
 32â†’     macos   | ---> nativeDarwin ---> native ---+
 33â†’     tvos    |                         ^
 34â†’     watchos /                         |
 35â†’                                       |
 36â†’     linux  \  ---> nativeOther -------+
 37â†’     mingw  /
 38â†’
 39â†’
 40â†’Explanation of JVM source sets structure:
 41â†’
 42â†’The overall structure is just a hack to support the scenario we are interested in:
 43â†’
 44â†’* We would like to have two source-sets "core" and "jdk8"
 45â†’* "jdk8" is allowed to use API from Java 8 and from "core"
 46â†’* "core" is prohibited to use any API from "jdk8"
 47â†’* It is okay to have tests in a single test source-set
 48â†’* And we want to publish a **single** artifact kotlinx-coroutines-core.jar that contains classes from both source-sets
 49â†’* Current limitation: only classes from "core" are checked with animal-sniffer
 50â†’
 51â†’For that, we have following compilations:
 52â†’* jvmMain compilation: [jvmCoreMain, jdk8Main]
 53â†’* jvmCore compilation: [commonMain]
 54â†’* jdk8    compilation: [commonMain, jvmCoreMain]
 55â†’
 56â†’Theoretically, "jvmCore" could've been "jvmMain", it is not for technical reasons,
 57â†’here is the explanation from Seb:
 58â†’
 59â†’"""
 60â†’The jvmCore is theoretically not necessary. All code for jdk6 compatibility can be in jvmMain and jdk8 dependent code can be in jdk8Main.
 61â†’Effectively there is no reason for ever putting code into jvmCoreMain.
 62â†’However, when creating a new compilation, we have to take care of creating a defaultSourceSet. Without creating the jvmCoreMain source set,
 63â†’ the creation of the compilation fails. That is the only reason for this source set.
 64â†’"""
 65â†’ ========================================================================== */
 66â†’
 67â†’kotlin {
 68â†’    sourceSets {
 69â†’        // using the source set names from <https://kotlinlang.org/docs/multiplatform-hierarchy.html#see-the-full-hierarchy-template>
 70â†’        groupSourceSets("concurrent", listOf("jvm", "native"), listOf("common"))
 71â†’        if (project.nativeTargetsAreEnabled) {
 72â†’            // TODO: 'nativeDarwin' behaves exactly like 'apple', we can remove it
 73â†’            groupSourceSets("nativeDarwin", listOf("apple"), listOf("native"))
 74â†’            groupSourceSets("nativeOther", listOf("linux", "mingw", "androidNative"), listOf("native"))
 75â†’        }
 76â†’        jvmMain {
 77â†’            dependencies {
 78â†’                compileOnly("com.google.android:annotations:4.1.1.4")
 79â†’            }
 80â†’        }
 81â†’        jvmTest {
 82â†’            dependencies {
 83â†’                api("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:lincheck:${version("lincheck")}")
 84â†’                api("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-knit-test:${version("knit")}")
 85â†’                implementation(project(":android-unit-tests"))
 86â†’                implementation("org.openjdk.jol:jol-core:0.16")
 87â†’            }
 88â†’        }
 89â†’    }
 90â†’    /*
 91â†’     * Configure two test runs for Native:
 92â†’     * 1) Main thread
 93â†’     * 2) BG thread (required for Dispatchers.Main tests on Darwin)
 94â†’     *
 95â†’     * All new MM targets are build with optimize = true to have stress tests properly run.
 96â†’     */
 97â†’    targets.withType(KotlinNativeTargetWithTests::class).configureEach {
 98â†’        binaries.test("workerTest", listOf(DEBUG)) {
 99â†’            val thisTest = this
100â†’            freeCompilerArgs = freeCompilerArgs + listOf("-e", "kotlinx.coroutines.mainBackground")
101â†’            testRuns.create("workerTest") {
102â†’                this as KotlinTaskTestRun<*, *>
103â†’                setExecutionSourceFrom(thisTest)
104â†’                executionTask.configure {
105â†’                    this as KotlinNativeTest
106â†’                    targetName = "$targetName worker with new MM"
107â†’                }
108â†’            }
109â†’        }
110â†’    }
111â†’
112â†’    /**
113â†’     * See: https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-25959
114â†’     * The introduction of jvmCore is only for CLI builds and not intended for the IDE.
115â†’     * In the current setup there are two tooling unfriendly configurations used:
116â†’     * 1: - jvmMain, despite being a platform source set, is not a leaf (jvmCoreMain and jdk8Main dependOn it)
117â†’     * 2: - jvmMain effectively becomes a 'shared jvm' source set
118â†’     *
119â†’     * Using this kludge here, will prevent issue 2 from being visible to the IDE.
120â†’     * Therefore jvmMain will resolve using the 'single' compilation it participates in (from the perspective of the IDE)
121â†’     */
122â†’    val jvmCoreMain = if (Idea.active) null else sourceSets.create("jvmCoreMain") {
123â†’        dependsOn(sourceSets.jvmMain.get())
124â†’    }
125â†’    val jdk8Main = sourceSets.create("jdk8Main") {
126â†’        dependsOn(sourceSets.jvmMain.get())
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    jvm {
130â†’        compilations.named("main") {

132â†’            source(jdk8Main)

134â†’
135â†’        /* Create compilation for jvmCore to prove that jvmMain does not rely on jdk8 */
136â†’        compilations.create("CoreMain") {
137â†’            /* jvmCore is automatically matched as 'defaultSourceSet' for the compilation, due to its name */
138â†’            tasks.getByName("check").dependsOn(compileTaskProvider)
139â†’        }
140â†’
141â†’        // For animal sniffer
142â†’        withJava()
143â†’        compilations.create("benchmark") { associateWith(this@jvm.compilations.getByName("main")) }
144â†’    }
145â†’}

115â†’/**

120â†’ * specified [context] using `coroutineContext + context` (see [CoroutineContext.plus]).
121â†’ * This suspending function is cancellable. It immediately checks for cancellation of
122â†’ * the resulting context and throws [CancellationException] if it is not [active][CoroutineContext.isActive].
123â†’ *

125â†’ * different from the current one, by necessity, perform additional dispatches: the [block]
126â†’ * can not be executed immediately and needs to be dispatched for execution on

128â†’ * has to shift back to the original dispatcher.

130â†’ * Note that the result of `withContext` invocation is dispatched into the original context in a cancellable way
131â†’ * with a **prompt cancellation guarantee**, which means that if the original [coroutineContext]
132â†’ * in which `withContext` was invoked is cancelled by the time its dispatcher starts to execute the code,
133â†’ * it discards the result of `withContext` and throws [CancellationException].
134â†’ *
135â†’ * The cancellation behaviour described above is enabled if and only if the dispatcher is being changed.
136â†’ * For example, when using `withContext(NonCancellable) { ... }` there is no change in dispatcher and
137â†’ * this call will not be cancelled neither on entry to the block inside `withContext` nor on exit from it.
138â†’ */
139â†’public suspend fun <T> withContext(
140â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
141â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
142â†’): T {
143â†’    contract {
144â†’        callsInPlace(block, InvocationKind.EXACTLY_ONCE)
145â†’    }
146â†’    return suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn sc@ { uCont ->
147â†’        // compute new context
148â†’        val oldContext = uCont.context
149â†’        // Copy CopyableThreadContextElement if necessary
150â†’        val newContext = oldContext.newCoroutineContext(context)
151â†’        // always check for cancellation of new context
152â†’        newContext.ensureActive()
153â†’        // FAST PATH #1 -- new context is the same as the old one
154â†’        if (newContext === oldContext) {
155â†’            val coroutine = ScopeCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
156â†’            @Suppress("LEAKED_IN_PLACE_LAMBDA") // Contract is preserved, invoked immediately or throws
157â†’            return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
158â†’        }
159â†’        // FAST PATH #2 -- the new dispatcher is the same as the old one (something else changed)
160â†’        // `equals` is used by design (see equals implementation is wrapper context like ExecutorCoroutineDispatcher)
161â†’        if (newContext[ContinuationInterceptor] == oldContext[ContinuationInterceptor]) {
162â†’            val coroutine = UndispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
163â†’            // There are changes in the context, so this thread needs to be updated
164â†’            withCoroutineContext(coroutine.context, null) {
165â†’                @Suppress("LEAKED_IN_PLACE_LAMBDA") // Contract is preserved, invoked immediately or throws
166â†’                return@sc coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturn(coroutine, block)
167â†’            }
168â†’        }
169â†’        // SLOW PATH -- use new dispatcher
170â†’        val coroutine = DispatchedCoroutine(newContext, uCont)
171â†’        @Suppress("LEAKED_IN_PLACE_LAMBDA")  // Contract is preserved, invoked immediately or throws
172â†’        block.startCoroutineCancellable(coroutine, coroutine)
173â†’        coroutine.getResult()
174â†’    }
175â†’}
176â†’
177â†’/**
178â†’ * Calls the specified suspending block with the given [CoroutineDispatcher], suspends until it
179â†’ * completes, and returns the result.
180â†’ *
181â†’ * This inline function calls [withContext].
182â†’ */
183â†’public suspend inline operator fun <T> CoroutineDispatcher.invoke(
184â†’    noinline block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
185â†’): T = withContext(this, block)
186â†’

188â†’
189â†’private open class StandaloneCoroutine(
190â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
191â†’    active: Boolean
192â†’) : AbstractCoroutine<Unit>(parentContext, initParentJob = true, active = active) {
193â†’    override fun handleJobException(exception: Throwable): Boolean {
194â†’        handleCoroutineException(context, exception)
195â†’        return true
196â†’    }
197â†’}
198â†’
199â†’private class LazyStandaloneCoroutine(
200â†’    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
201â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> Unit
202â†’) : StandaloneCoroutine(parentContext, active = false) {
203â†’    private val continuation = block.createCoroutineUnintercepted(this, this)
204â†’
205â†’    override fun onStart() {
206â†’        continuation.startCoroutineCancellable(this)
207â†’    }
208â†’}
209â†’
210â†’// Used by withContext when context changes, but dispatcher stays the same
211â†’internal expect class UndispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
212â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
213â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
214â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>
215â†’
216â†’private const val UNDECIDED = 0
217â†’private const val SUSPENDED = 1
218â†’private const val RESUMED = 2
219â†’
220â†’// Used by withContext when context dispatcher changes
221â†’internal class DispatchedCoroutine<in T>(
222â†’    context: CoroutineContext,
223â†’    uCont: Continuation<T>
224â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(context, uCont) {
225â†’    // this is copy-and-paste of a decision state machine inside AbstractionContinuation
226â†’    // todo: we may some-how abstract it via inline class
227â†’    private val _decision = atomic(UNDECIDED)
228â†’
229â†’    private fun trySuspend(): Boolean {
230â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
231â†’            when (decision) {
232â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, SUSPENDED)) return true
233â†’                RESUMED -> return false
234â†’                else -> error("Already suspended")
235â†’            }
236â†’        }
237â†’    }
238â†’
239â†’    private fun tryResume(): Boolean {
240â†’        _decision.loop { decision ->
241â†’            when (decision) {
242â†’                UNDECIDED -> if (this._decision.compareAndSet(UNDECIDED, RESUMED)) return true
243â†’                SUSPENDED -> return false
244â†’                else -> error("Already resumed")
245â†’            }
246â†’        }
247â†’    }
248â†’
249â†’    override fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {
250â†’        // Call afterResume from afterCompletion and not vice-versa, because stack-size is more
251â†’        // important for afterResume implementation
252â†’        afterResume(state)
253â†’    }
254â†’
255â†’    override fun afterResume(state: Any?) {
256â†’        if (tryResume()) return // completed before getResult invocation -- bail out
257â†’        // Resume in a cancellable way because we have to switch back to the original dispatcher
258â†’        uCont.intercepted().resumeCancellableWith(recoverResult(state, uCont))
259â†’    }
260â†’
261â†’    internal fun getResult(): Any? {
262â†’        if (trySuspend()) return COROUTINE_SUSPENDED
263â†’        // otherwise, onCompletionInternal was already invoked & invoked tryResume, and the result is in the state
264â†’        val state = this.state.unboxState()
265â†’        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) throw state.cause
266â†’        @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
267â†’        return state as T
268â†’    }
269â†’}
270â†’
